info.name: Midas DCF Valuation API
info.description:
REST API for equity valuation using Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis.

Computes intrinsic value per share from SEC filings, Yahoo Finance market data, and FRED macro indicators. Domestic 10-K filers and most foreign-private-issuer ADRs (TSM, ASML, BABA, …) are supported via the IFRS-FPI pipeline; per-ADR fair values are FX-converted to USD and divided by the configured depositary ratio so they compare like-for-like with the listed exchange price.

Every successful response carries `current_price`, `currency`, and `adr_ratio_applied` so consumers can compute the upside/downside discount without a second quote lookup.

variable.description: Default demo key created by `cmd/migrate`. For production, replace with a key minted via POST /api/v1/auth/keys.

item.name: Health
item.description: Health, readiness, and version endpoints. The first three are public; `/api/v1/health/detailed` requires `read:health`.

request.description: Basic liveness check. Returns 200 if the service process is running. No auth, no DB hit — safe to poll from a load-balancer.
Readiness check. Verifies the database is reachable and the cache is up. Use this for Kubernetes readiness probes.
Returns application version, environment, build time, and git commit. No auth required.
Per-component health: SEC EDGAR, Yahoo Finance, FRED, database, cache. Returns 200 (healthy), 206 (degraded), or 503 (unhealthy). Requires `read:health` permission.

item.name: Fair Value
item.description: DCF valuation endpoints. All require `read:fair_value`. Responses include `current_price` (live quote), `currency` (always USD post-conversion), `adr_ratio_applied` (1 for domestic; 5/8/10/… for ADRs), and `sanity_check` (DCF-implied multiples vs sector medians).

request.description: Calculate intrinsic fair value for a single domestic 10-K filer. Response includes `dcf_value_per_share`, `current_price` (live quote), `tangible_value_per_share`, plus WACC and growth assumptions. `adr_ratio_applied` is 1 for domestic filers.

Calculate fair value for a foreign-private-issuer ADR (Taiwan Semiconductor). Demonstrates the IFRS-FPI pipeline: SEC's IFRS-full XBRL is parsed, monetary values are FX-converted from TWD to USD via FRED, and ordinary-share counts are divided by the depositary ratio (TSM=5) so `dcf_value_per_share` and `current_price` are both per-ADR USD and directly comparable.

Note `adr_ratio_applied: 5` and `currency: "USD"` on the response.

query.description: Override beta for WACC calculation (0 - 3.0). Bypasses the cached Yahoo-derived beta — useful when you want to test sensitivity or skip cached data.
Override risk-free rate (0 - 0.20). Bypasses the FRED 10Y Treasury — useful for stress-testing under hypothetical rate scenarios.

request.description: Calculate fair value with custom beta and risk-free rate overrides. Setting either override also bypasses the response cache, which is the recommended way to force a fresh computation when debugging.

Calculate fair values for up to 10 tickers in one round-trip. HTTP status reflects outcome: 200 (all succeeded), 207 (partial — `failures` list explains per-ticker errors), or 422 (all failed). Each result has the same shape as the single-ticker response, including `current_price`, `currency`, and `adr_ratio_applied`.

response.name: 207 Multi-Status — Partial success

request.description:
Illustrates the `FOREIGN_PRIVATE_ISSUER_UNSUPPORTED` 422 returned for 20-F filers using a taxonomy or reporting currency the IFRS-FPI pipeline does not yet cover (JGAAP, K-IFRS, ifrs-smes, or currencies absent from FRED + `config/fx_rates.json`).

This is distinct from `INSUFFICIENT_DATA` — most ADRs (TSM, ASML, NVO, AZN, BABA, BIDU, TM, RIO, BHP, NVS, SHEL, BP) are fully supported and produce real per-ADR USD valuations.

response.name: 422 Unprocessable Entity — FPI unsupported
response.body:
{
  "type": "https://problems.midas.dev/FOREIGN_PRIVATE_ISSUER_UNSUPPORTED",
  "title": "Foreign private issuer not covered",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "This ticker files using a taxonomy or currency pair Midas does not yet cover. Supported: ifrs-full taxonomy with FRED-tracked currencies (TWD, EUR, JPY, GBP, HKD, CNY, KRW, CHF, CAD, AUD, INR, BRL, DKK).",
  "instance": "/api/v1/fair-value/SONY",
  "context": { "ticker": "SONY", "filing_type": "20-F", "taxonomy": "ifrs-full" },
  "code": "FOREIGN_PRIVATE_ISSUER_UNSUPPORTED",
  "timestamp": "2026-04-30T10:50:00Z",
  "method": "GET"
}

item.name: Metrics
item.description: Application and business metrics. Prometheus endpoint is public; the JSON application metrics require `read:metrics`.

request.description: Prometheus-format metrics exposition. Scrape this endpoint from your Prometheus server. No authentication required (lock down at the network layer if needed).
Application and business metrics in JSON form: request counts, valuation success/failure breakdowns, cache hit ratios, gateway latencies. Requires `read:metrics` permission.

item.name: Auth
item.description: API key management. Requires `manage:keys` permission. The raw key is only returned in the response body of `POST /keys` — store it immediately, the system only persists the hash.

request.description: Mint a new API key. The plaintext value is only returned once — capture it from the response body and store it in your secrets manager. Available permissions: `read:fair_value`, `read:health`, `read:metrics`, `manage:keys`, `admin`.
